macro_summary: Markets face a dense catalyst window with FOMC minutes dropping today (July 8, ~2pm ET), CPI on July 14, and Q2 earnings season kicking off July 13. Geopolitical risk has spiked materially after Trump declared the US-Iran ceasefire 'over' and confirmed fresh airstrikes, sending WTI crude +6.2% in premarket and pressuring inflation expectations. The semiconductor sector is recovering from a sharp early-July rout tied to DeepSeek in-house chip fears and Samsung weakness, even as AI capex forecasts remain robust at $650B+ for 2026. Fed rate-hike odds have edged up to 21.9% for later in 2026, keeping rate-sensitive tech valuations under scrutiny heading into a packed late-July earnings slate.
screening_note: 21 independent overnight screening runs analyzed; minimum threshold to qualify was 2 appearances. 7 tickers cleared the bar. Ideas ranked by appearance count descending.
key_events: FOMC June meeting minutes released today, July 8 (~2pm ET) — hawkish tone risk with 9/19 officials penciling in a hike
SK Hynix (SKHY) Nasdaq ADR begins trading July 10, 2026 — largest ADR debut in history (~$29.4B)
US-Iran ceasefire declared over by Trump; fresh US airstrikes confirmed; oil +6% premarket
June CPI print due July 14 — currently running 4.2% YoY, upside risk from energy shock
Q2 earnings season opens July 13; AAPL and MSFT fiscal earnings both expected late July (July 29–30)
NVDA fiscal Q2 FY2027 earnings August 25–26, 2026
Fed FOMC meeting July 28–29 — 21.9% hike probability priced in per CME FedWatch
Tariff expiry July 24 adds additional macro binary ahead of FOMC

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. American Depositary Shares
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 21 overnight screening runs — the strongest consensus signal in this digest. The Nasdaq ADR debut on July 10, 2026 is the largest in history (~$29.4B) and marks the first time US investors can directly access SK Hynix, the world's dominant HBM memory supplier with 50–70% market share and an order book sold out through 2027. HSBC applied a 38% Korean-listed price target upgrade (to ₩4M) using a 20% P/B premium for improved global accessibility, and the 'Korea Discount' removal plus mandatory index-fund buying post-listing create a structural re-rating catalyst unavailable before July 10.
ideas.catalyst: Nasdaq ADR first trading day: July 10, 2026 — 2 days away; cornerstone investors Coatue and Baillie Gifford committed ~$7B
ideas.edge: Trades at ~20.55x P/E vs. US semiconductor peers at 25–35x; HSBC 38% target uplift (₩2.9M → ₩4M); 'Korea Discount' removal unlocks re-rating for $1T+ HBM franchise previously inaccessible to US investors; index-fund forced buying post-listing
ideas.bull_case: ADR unlocks a structural re-rating to close the gap with US semi peers; HBM4 cycle and NVIDIA partnership provide durable earnings visibility with the order book locked through 2027, supporting multiple expansion toward 25–30x.
ideas.bear_case: Korean-listed shares already ran 250%+ YTD, meaning much of the re-rating may be priced in at the ADR opening; first-day price discovery risk is high on a $29.4B listing and options are unlikely to be available at open, limiting hedging flexibility.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in 20 of 21 overnight runs, making it the second-strongest consensus idea. Fiscal Q3 earnings on July 30, 2026 are a hard-dated catalyst with management guidance calling for 14–17% revenue growth, anchored by Q2's already-delivered 17% revenue growth ($111.2B) and 22% iPhone unit growth YoY. Foldable iPhone buzz ahead of a rumored fall reveal and AAPL's proximity to flipping NVDA for the largest global market cap (~$190B gap) add narrative momentum. The options structure — an August expiry call spread — offers defined-risk exposure to a pre-earnings run while capping downside from a 37x earnings valuation premium.
ideas.catalyst: Fiscal Q3 2026 earnings report: July 30, 2026; potential foldable iPhone ($2,500 price point) reveal in fall 2026
ideas.edge: Management guided 14–17% revenue growth with Q2 already delivering 17%; ~$190B market cap gap to NVDA that a strong print could close, triggering a re-rating narrative; cleaner near-term story than semis amid sector rotation
ideas.bull_case: A beat-and-raise on July 30 — feasible given Q2 momentum — combined with foldable iPhone hype could push AAPL past NVDA in market cap, triggering a media and index-rebalancing momentum wave into fall.
ideas.bear_case: At 37x earnings, AAPL is priced for near-perfection; any softness in Services revenue or China weakness (a recurring risk) could cause a sharp post-earnings de-rate, and the foldable narrative is unconfirmed.

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in 19 of 21 overnight runs and represents what screeners consistently flagged as the largest valuation dislocation among mega-cap tech. The stock is trading at its cheapest forward multiple (21–22x NTM EPS) since 2023 despite a 97-analyst bullish consensus and a $589–592 price target implying ~56% upside from current levels. The July 8 FOMC minutes and late-July fiscal Q4 earnings serve as sequential catalysts, while the launch of the $2.5B 'Frontier' AI deployment business (July 7) and 4,800-role layoffs signal margin improvement intent ahead of the print. A call spread into earnings captures the re-rating with defined downside risk from AI capex overhang.
ideas.catalyst: FY Q4 2026 earnings expected late July 2026; FOMC minutes today (July 8) as near-term macro read-through; $2.5B Frontier AI deployment business launched July 7
ideas.edge: 21–22x NTM P/E — cheapest since 2023; analyst consensus target $589–592 implies ~56% upside; Wolfe Research sees 34% upside independently; layoff-driven margin expansion reducing cost overhang ahead of print
ideas.bull_case: Azure acceleration in the AI era paired with cost discipline from layoffs and the new Frontier revenue line could catalyze a multiple re-expansion back toward 28–30x NTM, bridging most of the consensus target gap.
ideas.bear_case: AI infrastructure capex spend remains elevated and FCF conversion may disappoint; a hawkish FOMC tone today could compress multiples broadly across rate-sensitive growth stocks before earnings have a chance to re-rate.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in 15 of 21 overnight runs as the core AI infrastructure trade, currently in a post-rout recovery setup. The stock pulled back sharply in early July on DeepSeek in-house chip fears and Samsung weakness before rebounding July 7; screeners consistently flagged this as macro noise rather than a fundamental break. With 68 analysts averaging a $301 price target (53%+ upside), Q1 FY2027 revenue of $81.6B (+85% YoY), and the HBM4/Vera Rubin platform cycle ahead, August 25 earnings are the hard re-rating catalyst. A July 29 MSFT/GOOG earnings read-through on AI capex serves as a pre-catalyst setup. Options (Aug/Sep call spread) are preferred to define risk around the $198–203 resistance zone.
ideas.catalyst: Fiscal Q2 FY2027 earnings: August 25, 2026 (after market close); MSFT/GOOG AI capex read-through: July 29 as pre-catalyst; Kyber NVL144 delay rumor denied July 7
ideas.edge: Forward P/E of ~21.7x vs. 5-year average of 72x; 68-analyst consensus PT of $301 implies 53%+ upside; global chip sales +104% YoY in May 2026; AI capex guided $650B in 2026 rising to $1T in 2027
ideas.bull_case: If MSFT and GOOG confirm accelerating AI capex on July 29 and NVDA's August print shows continued 80%+ revenue growth, the historically compressed 21.7x forward multiple re-rates sharply, with Goldman flagging valuation as 'compelling' at current levels.
ideas.bear_case: The early-July selloff (-4.5% semis in a day) shows how quickly AI demand narrative fears can hit the stock; a hawkish FOMC surprise or softening MSFT Azure guidance on July 29 could pressure NVDA ahead of its own print.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust
ideas.thesis: QQQ appeared in 6 of 21 runs exclusively with medium conviction, flagged as a tactical hedge and defined-risk vehicle rather than a directional bet. The Nasdaq-100 fell 1.8% on July 7 during the semiconductor rout, and two binary macro events — FOMC minutes today (July 8) and June CPI on July 14 — land within six days. With CPI at 4.2% YoY and 9 of 19 Fed officials penciling in a hike, a hawkish minutes tone creates immediate downside risk for tech. S&P 500 Q2 earnings blended growth of 23.3% provides a longer-term bull floor, but the near-term vol window justifies a QQQ put spread or straddle for portfolio protection.
ideas.catalyst: FOMC minutes release: today, July 8 (~2pm ET); June CPI print: July 14, 2026; Q2 earnings season kickoff: July 13
ideas.edge: Nasdaq-100 down 1.8% July 7 — short-term dislocation vs. bullish H1 2026 trend; 78.1% Fed hold probability priced in but 9/19 officials lean hawkish — asymmetric surprise risk; semis down 4.5%+ in a single session shows concentrated vol in top QQQ holdings
ideas.bull_case: FOMC minutes confirm a data-dependent hold tone and CPI comes in at or below 4.2%; 23.3% blended Q2 EPS growth drives a tech re-rally into earnings season, making a call spread or long QQQ the winning trade.
ideas.bear_case: A hawkish minutes surprise today combined with a hotter-than-expected CPI on July 14 could extend the semiconductor-led selloff and pull the Nasdaq-100 back toward its early-July lows, making put spreads the payoff scenario.

ideas.name: Energy Select Sector SPDR ETF
ideas.thesis: XLE appeared in 6 of 21 runs, consistently identified as the cleanest sector rotation trade in today's environment. Trump declared the US-Iran ceasefire 'over' this morning with confirmed fresh US airstrikes, sending WTI crude +6.2% and Brent +6.1% in premarket — a discrete, hard-dated supply shock with direct pass-through to energy earnings. XLE is up ~20% YTD and still offers FCF yields above 10% and dividend yields above 3%, while the 10-year Treasury yield surging to 4.58% further supports energy over rate-sensitive sectors. The ETF provides diversified, liquid, optionable exposure to the geopolitical premium without single-stock concentration risk.
ideas.catalyst: US declares Iran ceasefire over and resumes military strikes: July 8, 2026; US revocation of Iran oil export waiver effective July 7–8; IMF projects oil up ~32% for full-year 2026
ideas.edge: WTI +6.2% and Brent +6.1% intraday on confirmed escalation; energy is the only major sector with a direct positive fundamental tailwind today; XOM (22% weight) and CVX (16% weight) are integrated supermajors with low breakeven costs; FCF yields >10% provide earnings support even at current oil levels
ideas.bull_case: Strait of Hormuz disruption risk and sustained Iran sanctions remove material supply from the market; oil remains elevated through summer, XLE earnings estimates reset higher, and sector rotation out of tech accelerates the ETF's outperformance.
ideas.bear_case: Geopolitical premiums in oil are historically mean-reverting; a diplomatic de-escalation or OPEC+ production response could quickly reverse today's oil spike, and XLE's 20% YTD run already prices in significant geopolitical risk.

ideas.name: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust
ideas.thesis: SPY appeared in only 2 of 21 runs, just clearing the minimum threshold, and was flagged exclusively as a hedge overlay rather than a directional trade. Three overlapping macro catalysts — FOMC minutes today (July 8), tariff expiry July 24, and the FOMC decision July 29 — create a dense risk window. With Brent crude spiking 5.85%+ on Iran escalation adding fresh inflation risk and Fed futures pricing 1.5 hikes over 12 months (a sharp reversal from prior cut expectations), a short-dated SPY put spread (July/August expiry) offers low-cost protection for tech-heavy swing portfolios against a hawkish surprise or geopolitical shock.
ideas.catalyst: FOMC June minutes released today July 8 (~2pm ET); tariff expiry July 24; FOMC rate decision July 29 — 21.9% probability of hike per CME FedWatch
ideas.edge: 21.9% hike probability is an underpriced tail risk given 9/19 Fed officials penciling in a hike; Iran oil spike adds inflation surprise risk ahead of July 14 CPI; three macro binaries in 16 days compress the hedge window, keeping put spread premiums relatively contained
ideas.bull_case: FOMC minutes confirm a patient, data-dependent hold; CPI and tariff outcomes are benign; Q2 23.3% EPS growth carries the index through the catalyst window and put spreads expire worthless as insurance.
ideas.bear_case: A hawkish minutes tone today combined with an oil-driven CPI upside surprise and a hike signal at the July 29 FOMC meeting could produce a 3–5% index drawdown in a compressed timeframe, validating the put spread hedge.
